data.title: The WSJ Dollar Index Falls 0.72% This Week to 95.74 — Data Talk
data.description:
The WSJ Dollar Index is down 0.70 point or 0.72% this week to 95.74

  • Largest one-week point and percentage decline since the week ending June 27, 2025
  • Down four of the past five weeks
  • Today it is up 0.11 point or 0.12%
  • Largest one-day point and percentage gain since Friday, Dec. 19, 2025
  • Snaps a four-trading-day losing streak
  • Off 8.94% from its record close of 105.14 hit Tuesday, Sept. 27, 2022
  • Off 7.59% from its 52-week high of 103.61 hit Friday, Jan. 10, 2025
  • Up 1.95% from its 52-week low of 93.91 hit Tuesday, July 1, 2025
  • Down 6.54% from 52 weeks ago
  • Month-to-date it is down 1.17%
  • Year-to-date it is down 7.01 points or 6.82%

Data based on 5 p.m. ET values

Source: Tullett Prebon and Dow Jones Market Data

data.title: Corn, soy basis bids flat
data.description:
U.S. corn and soybean basis bids held steady at grain elevators, ethanol plants, river and rail terminals and other kinds of processors in a spot check of facilities in the U.S. Midwest on Friday as futures ticked down.

  • Futures have been thinly traded following Thursday's Christmas holiday and ahead of the weekend.

  • Soybean futures were pressured by large global supplies.

  • Strong demand for U.S. corn offered the commodity some support on a day of choppy trade.

  • CBOT March corn (CH26) settled 1 cent lower at $4.50 per bushel. CBOT January soybeans (SF26) settled 4-1/2 cents lower at $10.58-3/4 per bushel.

Basis values are quoted against CBOT futures in cents/bu:
